name: typescript-patterns
description: TypeScript 5.9.3 strict mode patterns and conventions for the claude-toad CLI codebase
globs: ["src/**/*.ts", "tests/**/*.ts", "tsconfig.json"]

> **When to read this skill:** Before editing, creating, or reviewing any `.ts` files in `src/` or `tests/`, or when modifying `tsconfig.json`. Read this skill completely before writing any new TypeScript module or refactoring existing type definitions.

## TypeScript 5.9.3 Strict Mode — claude-toad Conventions

### Compiler Strictness

This project runs full strict mode. Every flag is on. No exceptions.

```typescript
// tsconfig.json enforces these — never relax them
"strict": true
// This means all of: strictNullChecks, strictFunctionTypes,
// strictBindCallApply, strictPropertyInitialization,
// noImplicitAny, noImplicitThis, alwaysStrict
```

### Type Annotation Rules

Every function must have explicit parameter types AND return types. No inference for function signatures.

```typescript
// ✅ Correct — explicit return type
function scanProject(rootDir: string): Promise<ProjectAnalysis> {
  // ...
}

// ✅ Correct — arrow functions too
const parseConfig = (filePath: string): ConfigResult => {
  // ...
};

// ❌ Wrong — implicit return type
function scanProject(rootDir: string) {
  // ...
}

// ❌ Wrong — no parameter types
const parseConfig = (filePath) => {
  // ...
};
```

### Interface vs Type

Use `interface` for object shapes (data structures, configs, API responses). Use `type` for unions, intersections, mapped types, and utility types.

```typescript
// ✅ Object shapes → interface
interface ProjectAnalysis {
  framework: FrameworkInfo;
  language: LanguageInfo;
  structure: ProjectStructure;
}

interface ScanResult {
  files: string[];
  configs: Map<string, unknown>;
}

// ✅ Unions, intersections, utilities → type
type Command = 'init' | 'new' | 'package' | 'add-skill';
type SkillSource = YouTubeSource | PdfSource | UrlSource | AudioSource;
type Nullable<T> = T | null;
type DeepPartial<T> = { [P in keyof T]?: DeepPartial<T[P]> };
```

### No `any` — Ever

```typescript
// ❌ Never
const data: any = JSON.parse(raw);

// ✅ Use unknown and narrow
const data: unknown = JSON.parse(raw);
if (isProjectConfig(data)) {
  // now typed
}

// ✅ Or use a specific type with a type guard
function isProjectConfig(value: unknown): value is ProjectConfig {
  return typeof value === 'object' && value !== null && 'name' in value;
}
```

### Error Handling

Throw typed errors. Never swallow exceptions. Isolate side effects to I/O boundaries.

```typescript
// ✅ Define specific error types
class ScanError extends Error {
  constructor(
    message: string,
    public readonly filePath: string,
    public readonly cause?: Error
  ) {
    super(message);
    this.name = 'ScanError';
  }
}

class ApiKeyError extends Error {
  constructor(public readonly source: 'env' | 'config') {
    super(`API key not found in ${source}`);
    this.name = 'ApiKeyError';
  }
}

// ❌ Never swallow errors
try {
  await scanProject(dir);
} catch {
  // silent failure — NEVER do this
}

// ✅ Handle or re-throw with context
try {
  await scanProject(dir);
} catch (error) {
  throw new ScanError(`Failed to scan ${dir}`, dir, error as Error);
}
```

### Import Style

Explicit named imports only. No wildcard imports. No default exports unless required by tooling.

```typescript
// ✅ Named imports
import { readFile, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
import { resolve, join } from 'node:path';
import { ProjectAnalysis, ScanResult } from './types.js';

// ❌ No wildcard imports
import * as fs from 'node:fs/promises';
import * as path from 'node:path';

// ❌ No barrel re-exports that pull in the world
import { everything } from './index.js';
```

### File Naming

All source files use kebab-case. No exceptions.

```
src/project-scanner.ts    ✅
src/api-client.ts         ✅
src/skill-generator.ts    ✅
src/ProjectScanner.ts     ❌
src/apiClient.ts          ❌
src/skill_generator.ts    ❌
```

### Async Patterns

Always `async/await`. Never raw `.then()` chains.

```typescript
// ✅ async/await
async function loadConfig(configPath: string): Promise<ToadConfig> {
  const raw = await readFile(configPath, 'utf-8');
  const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(raw);
  return validateConfig(parsed);
}

// ❌ Promise chains
function loadConfig(configPath: string): Promise<ToadConfig> {
  return readFile(configPath, 'utf-8')
    .then((raw) => JSON.parse(raw))
    .then((parsed) => validateConfig(parsed));
}
```

### Pure Functions for Analysis

The scan phase is pure — no side effects, no API calls. Keep computation separate from I/O.

```typescript
// ✅ Pure analysis function — takes data, returns data
function detectFramework(packageJson: PackageJson): FrameworkInfo | null {
  const deps = { ...packageJson.dependencies, ...packageJson.devDependencies };
  if ('next' in deps) return { name: 'nextjs', version: deps['next'] };
  if ('nuxt' in deps) return { name: 'nuxt', version: deps['nuxt'] };
  return null;
}

// ✅ I/O isolated at boundaries
async function scan(rootDir: string): Promise<ProjectAnalysis> {
  const packageJson = await readPackageJson(rootDir);  // I/O boundary
  const framework = detectFramework(packageJson);       // Pure
  const structure = analyzeStructure(fileList);          // Pure
  return buildAnalysis(framework, structure);            // Pure
}
```

### Node.js Built-in Imports

Always use the `node:` protocol prefix for Node.js built-ins.

```typescript
// ✅ node: prefix
import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
import { join } from 'node:path';
import { execSync } from 'node:child_process';

// ❌ No prefix
import { readFile } from 'fs/promises';
```
